name: Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Navia De Suarna traverse a diverse landscape within the Ancares region of Galicia, Spain. The area features a dramatic combination of mountain peaks and wide valleys, with the Navia River and its tributaries carving through the terrain. Dense forests of oak, chestnut, and birch cover much of the region, contributing to the challenging and varied riding conditions. This environment provides a robust network of trails suitable for experienced mountain bikers seeking demanding routes. Best mountain bike trails around Navia De Suarna The most popular mountain bike trail is Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada, a difficult 40.1 miles (64.5 km) trail that takes about 6 hours 34 minutes to complete. This challenging route navigates through the Eo River Biosphere Reserve and Terras de Burón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra, a difficult 19.3 miles (31.1 km) path. This circular ride follows the course of the Navia River, offering varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail, a 22.7 miles (36.5 km) trail leading through the Navia river valley, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Navia De Suarna is defined by dramatic mountain peaks, wide river valleys, and dense forests. The network offers options primarily for advanced riders seeking challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navia De Suarna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Navia de Suarna?
A: The mountain bike trails around Navia de Suarna are primarily designed for experienced riders. All five routes listed in this guide are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and technical terrain. Riders should have good fitness and advanced skills, as some sections may require pushing your bike.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or beginner mountain bike trails in Navia de Suarna?
A: Navia de Suarna's mountain biking routes are generally challenging and best suited for experienced riders. The trails often involve technical paths and rugged terrain, making them less ideal for beginners or families with young children seeking an easy ride. The region is more geared towards those looking for demanding routes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Navia de Suarna?
A: You'll experience a dramatic and varied landscape, characterized by magical mountain peaks, wide valleys, and dense forests of oak, chestnut, and birch. The Navia River and its tributaries carve through the terrain, offering lush green valleys and clear rivers. The Ancares region, where Navia de Suarna is located, is renowned for its natural beauty and diverse ecosystem.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning natural attractions. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mostallar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedras Apañadas Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancares Mountains&lt;/a&gt; themselves are a significant natural monument, and you might even encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Navia de Suarna is rich in cultural heritage. You can explore close to 25 Celtic forts, offering glimpses into ancient settlements. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7529413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Proba&lt;/a&gt; stone bridge over the Navia River is a famous landmark. Additionally, traditional architecture, including 'pallozas ancareñas' (ancient circular dwellings), can be found in mountain hamlets, and the medieval castle and bridge in A Pobra are significant monumental attractions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, all the featured mountain bike routes in this guide are circular. For example, you can ride the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210343234/alto-de-pedras-apanadas-desde-fonsagrada-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 64.5 km loop, or the 31.1 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210654475/paradela-y-penamil-desde-a-pobra-circular-del-rio-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra&lt;/a&gt; along the Navia River.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navia de Suarna?
A: The spring and summer months are ideal for mountain biking in Navia de Suarna. During this time, the meadows burst with colorful wildflowers, and the lush environment is at its peak. The weather is generally more favorable, allowing for comfortable rides through the valleys and mountains. While the region is beautiful year-round, some higher mountain sections might be affected by snow in winter.

Q: Can I use an e-bike on the trails in Navia de Suarna?
A: Yes, the Navia de Suarna area is supportive of e-bike riding, and numerous trails allow their use. An e-bike can be particularly helpful on some of the more demanding routes with significant elevation gains, allowing you to enjoy the challenging terrain with a bit of assistance.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210343234/alto-de-pedras-apanadas-desde-fonsagrada-circular-por-la-reserva-de-biosfera-rio-eo-y-terras-de-buron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pedras Apañadas desde Fonsagrada&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 64.5 km route, typically taking over 6 hours. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 63.7 km through the Sierra de Los Ancares.

Q: Are there places to rest or picnic along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for refreshing breaks. You can find picnic areas along some routes, particularly near the Navia River. These spots provide a chance to rest, refuel, and enjoy the beautiful natural surroundings.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Navia de Suarna?
A: Mountain bikers who have explored Navia de Suarna often praise the dramatic mountain peaks, wide river valleys, and dense forests that define the region. The challenging nature of the trails is a significant draw for advanced riders seeking an exhilarating experience amidst stunning natural beauty.

Q: What are some shorter, yet still challenging, mountain bike routes?
A: If you're looking for a challenging ride that's a bit shorter, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210394950/bustelin-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bustelín Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great option at 36.5 km, typically completed in under 4 hours. Another choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210459401/quinta-rio-donsal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintá-Río Donsal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.2 km circular ride.
tips: A magical arched bridge in the medieval village of Navia de Suarna (A Proba) over the Navia river. Great place to start your adventure. Don't forget to go swimming after the ride in the natural pools down the river.

waypoints.name: Medieval Bridge of Navia de Suarna
